texto: For no matter how many battles had been won or lost, no matter how many friends and soldiers killed, every battle felt like the first. And I realized that it wasn't the training, nor the pain of seeing friends die, nor the will to win that made the men fight, but their will to survive that made them soldiers.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Magus Guidan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “For no matter how many battles had been won or lost, no matter how many friends and soldiers kill…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, soldiers, survival pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e termino.
